methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: User approves assetToken to Factory
proposeAgent transfers applicationThreshold from user
Application stored in _applications mapping
executeApplication or withdraw called
On execute: tokens deployed, LP created, NFT minted, TBA created
On withdraw: assetToken returned to proposer
transformations: withdrawableAmount used as initial LP liquidity
data_flows.trust_changes: User funds held by factory with no timelock; proposer or WITHDRAW_ROLE can execute/withdraw
exposure_risks: WITHDRAW_ROLE can execute or withdraw any application regardless of proposer intent
manipulation_risks: WITHDRAW_ROLE holder can front-run proposer's withdraw with executeApplication, converting their deposit into LP without consent

path: transfer/transferFrom called with LP address involvement
_transfer detects applyTax = true
_taxProcessing computes tax and accumulates to contract balance + projectTaxPendingSwap
_autoSwap checks threshold and swaps accumulated tax tokens to pairToken via Uniswap
Swap proceeds sent directly to projectTaxRecipient
transformations: tax cast to uint128 and added to projectTaxPendingSwap
data_flows.trust_changes: Tax recipient set by owner/factory; no validation of recipient address
validation_points: _eligibleForSwap checks threshold, no autoswap in progress, not from LP/router
exposure_risks: amountOutMin = 0 in swap allows full sandwich attack extraction
Failed swap silently caught, tax tokens accumulate indefinitely
manipulation_risks: MEV bots can sandwich the autoswap getting 100% of swap value as slippage
Owner can change projectTaxRecipient to divert accumulated tax tokens
Owner can set tax rates arbitrarily high with no maximum check in setProjectTaxRates

path: supplyParams decoded (maxSupply, lpSupply, vaultSupply)
_processSupplyParams validates maxSupply == lpSupply + vaultSupply
_mintBalances mints lpSupply to address(this), vaultSupply to vault
_mint casts amount to uint128 for _totalSupply
data_flows.trust_changes: Factory is trusted to provide correct supply params
exposure_risks: uint128 truncation in _mint if amount exceeds uint128 despite check
manipulation_risks: Admin controls _tokenSupplyParams and can set arbitrary values for new tokens

path: User approves assetToken (LP token) to VeToken
stake transfers LP from sender to VeToken contract
_mint creates veToken for receiver
_delegate sets receiver's delegate to delegatee
_balanceCheckpoints updated
addValidator called on AgentNft for delegatee
transformations: Delegation overrides any previous delegation entirely
data_flows.trust_changes: Anyone can register as validator via public addValidator on AgentNftV2
exposure_risks: Re-staking overrides delegation for ENTIRE balance, not just new amount
Public addValidator allows anyone to become validator
manipulation_risks: Attacker can call addValidator on AgentNftV2 to register as validator without staking
First staker's initialLock locks founder's tokens until maturity

path: User provides external ERC20 token address and initialLP amount
Factory checks _tokenApplication[tokenAddr] == 0 (sentinel value check)
Transfers initialLP of custom token and applicationThreshold of assetToken
On execute: creates Uniswap pair, adds liquidity with both tokens
On withdraw: returns assetToken and ALL of that custom token balance in factory
transformations: _tokenApplication[tokenAddr] = id maps token to application ID
Custom token liquidity paired with assetToken on Uniswap
data_flows.trust_changes: isCompatibleToken only checks basic ERC20 interface, not behavior
exposure_risks: Sentinel value 0 collision with potential application ID 0
withdraw sends entire factory balance of custom token, not just deposited amount
manipulation_risks: Malicious token with hooks could reenter during safeTransferFrom
If _nextId starts at 0, first application ID is 0, making _tokenApplication mapping check unreliable

path: Founder transfers initialAmount of assetToken to new token contract
New AgentToken, VeToken, and DAO deployed
AgentNft.migrateVirtual updates on-chain records
LP tokens staked via VeToken
transformations: Old DAO voting params copied to new DAO
data_flows.trust_changes: migrateVirtual requires ADMIN_ROLE on AgentNft; AgentMigrator must have this role
exposure_risks: Uses raw transferFrom instead of safeTransferFrom for assetToken
Owner can reset migration flag and re-migrate
manipulation_risks: Owner can call reset() to allow re-migration, potentially creating duplicate agent infrastructure

core_rules: Applications require applicationThreshold deposit of assetToken
Applications can be executed (creating agent infrastructure) or withdrawn (returning deposit)
AgentTokens have buy/sell tax collected and auto-swapped via Uniswap
VeTokens are non-transferable and represent staked LP positions with delegation
Founder's initial stake is locked until maturity (matureAt timestamp)
Each agent has NFT, DAO, Token, VeToken, TBA, and LP pool
Validators are registered per-virtualId with ELO-based scoring
economic_incentives: Application threshold acts as spam prevention and provides initial liquidity
Tax on buy/sell funds the project tax recipient (treasury/creator/TBA)
Staking LP tokens gives governance power through VeToken delegation
Validators earn score based on DAO participation (proposal voting)
governance_mechanisms: Agent DAO governs agent metadata and core types
VeToken holders delegate to validators who vote on proposals
DAO can set new DAO address via setDAO
Validator scores combine base score (from initial registration) + DAO participation score

edge_cases.scenario: AgentToken tax processing uses unchecked block with uint128 cast for projectTaxPendingSwap
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If projectTax exceeds uint128.max (practically unlikely but theoretically possible with high supply and high tax), silent truncation occurs. More realistically, accumulated projectTaxPendingSwap can overflow uint128 over many transactions in the unchecked block
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: With very high volume and tax rates, projectTaxPendingSwap could silently wrap around uint128, causing accounting desync between actual contract balance and tracked pending swap amount

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only stakers who delegate to a validator should register them
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Anyone can call addValidator(virtualId, anyAddress) to register any address as a validator for any agent
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker registers many addresses as validators, diluting validator scores and manipulating totalUptimeScore. Since _initValidatorScore grants base score equal to totalProposals at registration time, registering after many proposals gives free score

edge_cases.scenario: AgentToken.setProjectTaxRates has no maximum or monotonicity enforcement
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Comment says 'subject to only ever decreasing' - tax rates should only decrease
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Owner or factory can set any uint16 value including increasing rates up to 65535 basis points (655%)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Owner/factory can set sell tax to 100% (10000 BP) or higher, effectively stealing all tokens from sellers. Tax above 100% would cause underflow in unchecked block

edge_cases.scenario: AgentFactoryV4 sentinel value collision for _tokenApplication mapping
edge_cases.expected_behavior: _tokenApplication[addr] == 0 should reliably indicate no application exists
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If _nextId is initialized to 0, the first application gets ID 0, making _tokenApplication[tokenAddr] = 0 indistinguishable from 'no application'
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Could allow the same token to be registered twice if first application has ID 0, or prevent proper cleanup on withdrawal

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Tax swaps should receive fair market value
edge_cases.actual_behavior: swapExactTokensForTokensSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens called with amountOutMin=0, accepting any output amount
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: MEV bots can sandwich every autoswap transaction, front-running to manipulate price, then back-running to capture the price difference. All accumulated tax value can be extracted

edge_cases.scenario: AgentFactoryV2 uses IERC20.transfer instead of SafeERC20.safeTransfer
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Transfer should be verified for success
edge_cases.actual_behavior: IERC20(assetToken).transfer(token, initialAmount) does not use SafeERC20 wrapper
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If assetToken is a non-standard ERC20 that returns false instead of reverting on failure, the transfer would silently fail and executeApplication would proceed with no initial liquidity

edge_cases.scenario: AgentFactoryV4.withdraw transfers entire factory balance of custom token
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should return only the amount deposited by the withdrawer
edge_cases.actual_behavior: IERC20(customToken).safeTransfer(application.proposer, IERC20(customToken).balanceOf(address(this))) sends ALL tokens of that type held by the factory
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If custom tokens arrive at the factory through other means (airdrops, direct transfers, dust), the withdrawer receives all of them. Also, if factory holds other legitimate custom token balances for other operations, they would be drained

edge_cases.scenario: AgentVeToken.stake delegation overrides entire balance delegation
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Additional stakes should allow split delegation or additive behavior
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _delegate(receiver, delegatee) replaces delegation for the ENTIRE receiver balance, not just the newly staked amount
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A user who has staked and delegated to validator A, then stakes 1 wei and delegates to validator B, moves their entire voting power from A to B instantly

edge_cases.scenario: AgentNftV2.setDAO can set DAO to address(0)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: DAO should always be a valid governance contract
edge_cases.actual_behavior: No validation on newDAO address; setting to address(0) permanently locks governance since onlyVirtualDAO modifier would require msg.sender == address(0)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Malicious DAO proposal could set DAO to address(0), permanently disabling governance for that agent

edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any address can call distributeTaxTokens to force direct transfer of pending tax tokens instead of swap
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Could be used to avoid the Uniswap swap step, sending raw agent tokens to projectTaxRecipient instead of converted pairToken. May disrupt expected revenue flow

edge_cases.scenario: botProtectionDurationInSeconds is stored but never enforced
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Bot protection should restrict early trading after funding
edge_cases.actual_behavior: The variable is set in initialize but never referenced in transfer logic or any other function
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Bots can freely trade immediately after initial liquidity is added, sniping the launch with no protection

edge_cases.scenario: Tax rate can exceed BP_DENOM (10000) causing underflow in unchecked block
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Tax should be a fraction of the transaction amount
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If projectSellTaxBasisPoints is set > 10000, tax = (sentAmount_ * projectSellTaxBasisPoints) / BP_DENOM would exceed sentAmount_. In the unchecked block, amountLessTax_ -= tax would underflow to a huge number
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Owner sets tax > 100%, sells trigger underflow making amountLessTax_ extremely large, potentially draining the contract or causing massive token inflation to receiver

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should return sum of all validator scores
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Iterates over all validators for a virtualId; with many validators (from public addValidator) this can exceed block gas limit
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker registers thousands of validators via public addValidator, making totalUptimeScore and any functions depending on it revert due to gas limits

assumption_violations: Tax rates assumed to be <= BP_DENOM (10000) but not enforced in setProjectTaxRates
Application IDs assumed to be non-zero for sentinel value checks in V4 but _nextId can start at 0
addValidator in AgentNftV2 assumed to only be called by staking flow but is public with no access control
botProtectionDurationInSeconds assumed to provide protection but is never used
AssetToken assumed to be standard ERC20 but V2 factory uses raw transfer instead of SafeERC20
_totalSupply assumed to fit uint128 but _balances are uint256, creating potential accounting mismatch
Autoswap assumes fair market execution but uses 0 slippage protection
game_theory_risks: MEV extraction on every tax autoswap due to zero slippage protection - systematic value extraction from all token holders
Owner/factory can change tax rates and recipient at any time, effectively a centralized rug-pull vector
Anyone can grief totalUptimeScore by registering thousands of dummy validators via public addValidator
WITHDRAW_ROLE can execute applications without proposer consent, forcing proposer's funds into LP
Validator score gaming: register as validator after many proposals to get high base score for free
Delegation override on re-stake allows instant voting power reallocation without unstaking
distributeTaxTokens can be called by anyone to bypass Uniswap swap, sending raw tokens to tax recipient
integration_risks: Uniswap V2 router interaction in autoswap has no slippage protection (amountOutMin = 0)
ERC6551 TBA creation depends on external registry; if implementation is malicious, TBA could be compromised
Clone-based deployment (Clones.clone) creates minimal proxies that cannot be upgraded individually
Uniswap pair creation in _createPair checks getPair == address(0) but race condition possible if pair created externally between check and create
AgentToken approves Uniswap router for type(uint256).max - infinite approval persists even if router is compromised
Fee-on-transfer tokens used as pairToken would cause addLiquidity accounting discrepancies
calculation_errors: AgentToken._mint: _totalSupply += uint128(amount) silently truncates amounts exceeding uint128, while _balances[account] += amount uses full uint256
AgentToken._burn: _totalSupply -= uint128(amount) could underflow differently than expected if balance tracking diverged from supply
AgentToken._taxProcessing: entire block is unchecked; amountLessTax_ -= tax can underflow if tax > sentAmount_ (possible with tax BP > 10000)

AgentToken._swapTax: proportional reduction formula projectTaxPendingSwap -= uint128((projectTaxPendingSwap * swapBalance_) / contractBalance_) may lose precision and leave dust in projectTaxPendingSwap
_initValidatorScore sets base score to totalProposals at registration time, giving late-registering validators an unfair advantage equal to all past proposals

token_semantics.notes: Custom ERC20 with buy/sell tax via liquidity pool detection. Tax tokens auto-swapped to pairToken. Unchecked arithmetic in tax processing. _totalSupply uses uint128 but _balances use uint256. botProtectionDurationInSeconds set but never enforced.
Non-transferable vote-escrowed token. transfer/transferFrom/approve all revert. 1:1 minting from LP staking. Delegation checkpointed via AddressCheckpoints. Founder has initialLock until maturity.
External base currency token used for application deposits and LP pairing. V2 factory uses raw IERC20.transfer (unsafe). V3/V4 use SafeERC20.
Standard Uniswap V2 LP token. Used as staking asset for AgentVeToken. Raw IERC20.transfer used in _addInitialLiquidity.

invariants.name: supply_conservation_agent_token
invariants.notes: BROKEN: _totalSupply uses uint128 cast while _balances use uint256. If minted amounts are large, totalSupply could be truncated while balances are accurate, breaking conservation.

invariants.name: application_status_finality
invariants.notes: Application should transition Active -> Executed or Active -> Withdrawn exactly once. Enforced by status check.

invariants.name: tax_accounting
invariants.notes: projectTaxPendingSwap should be <= balanceOf(address(this)). Possible desync due to uint128 truncation in accumulation and precision loss in proportional reduction during partial swaps.

invariants.name: ve_token_supply_backing
invariants.notes: VeToken total supply should equal LP tokens held by contract. Maintained by 1:1 mint on stake and burn on withdraw.

invariants.name: validator_uniqueness
invariants.notes: Public addValidator checks isValidator before adding. But internal _addValidator (called in mint) doesn't check, allowing potential duplicate in array though map stays correct.

invariants.name: token_application_mapping_consistency
invariants.notes: V4: bidirectional mapping uses 0 as sentinel. If application ID 0 exists, mapping breaks. Cleared on withdraw but not on execute.
